title: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values
body:
It looks like in some cases setup_preload gets called more than once, so it was adding `libscalene.so` to `LD_PRELOAD` the first time then later replacing `LD_PRELOAD`  with just `libscalene.so`, this fixes that case.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix Linux LD_PRELOAD handling so repeated calls to ScalenePreload.setup_preload() do not overwrite or collapse existing LD_PRELOAD entries when libscalene.so is already present. The change makes preload environment construction and restart checks stable and idempotent across multiple invocations.

steps.operation: Make LD_PRELOAD merging idempotent by tokenizing existing LD_PRELOAD, detecting any libscalene.so entry (including full paths), de-duplicating, and prepending libscalene.so while preserving all other entries and a stable join format across repeated calls
steps.reason: The bug arises when setup_preload is invoked multiple times and get_preload_environ reconstructs LD_PRELOAD in a way that can drop previously-existing entries or collapse to only libscalene.so. A stable, token-based merge prevents overwrites and guarantees that subsequent calls produce the same LD_PRELOAD string (idempotency), avoiding both data loss and restart loops.
steps.side_effects: Changes the exact LD_PRELOAD string formatting (ordering/delimiters) which may affect downstream string-comparison logic; should be validated on Linux with various existing LD_PRELOAD formats (space-separated, colon-separated, mixed). May also affect restart behavior if other code assumes a specific delimiter.

steps.operation: Adjust environment-diff / restart decision to avoid re-exec when the process already satisfies the required preload state, treating LD_PRELOAD equivalence in a normalized way (e.g., compare token sets/order-stable normalized string) and ensuring a second call to setup_preload does not rewrite or re-exec unnecessarily
steps.reason: Even with correct merging, brittle exact string comparisons can cause setup_preload to think the environment differs and trigger an unnecessary restart (or rewrite LD_PRELOAD). Normalizing LD_PRELOAD comparison (and/or skipping updates when libscalene.so is already present alongside other tokens) ensures repeated calls are no-ops and preserves user/system preload entries.
steps.side_effects: May reduce the frequency of re-exec in edge cases where environment values differ only by benign formatting; ensure this does not suppress required restarts when LD_PRELOAD is genuinely missing or incorrect. Add/adjust tests (if present) or validate via manual scenarios: 'foo.so bar.so' -> prepend; 'libscalene.so foo.so' unchanged; '/path/libscalene.so foo.so' unchanged; calling setup_preload twice should not change env nor re-exec.
